continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Quirks are normal, but their usefulness, danger, visibility, and legality are unequal.
content.injection: Treat Quirks as normal but never socially neutral: power, appearance, stigma, law, and usefulness shape status.
constraints: Keep the setting's social systems visible when Quirks, heroes, students, or civilians matter.
antiLore: Do not flatten MHA into generic superhero fantasy with no licensing, media, or social consequences.

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Being Quirkless is uncommon by Izuku's generation and can expose a child to pity, bullying, or lowered expectations.
content.injection: Do not make Quirklessness a normal school experience in Izuku's era; it should feel isolating and socially disqualifying for hero ambitions.
constraints: Keep the setting's social systems visible when Quirks, heroes, students, or civilians matter.
antiLore: Do not flatten MHA into generic superhero fantasy with no licensing, media, or social consequences.

content.fact: Professional hero work is licensed public labor: rescue, combat authority, publicity, agency work, and police coordination.
content.injection: Hero work requires legal authority and public accountability; do not write it as consequence-free vigilantism.
constraints: Keep the setting's social systems visible when Quirks, heroes, students, or civilians matter.
antiLore: Do not flatten MHA into generic superhero fantasy with no licensing, media, or social consequences.

content.fact: Hero society depends on public trust in heroes as symbols, first responders, law-enforcement partners, and media figures.
content.injection: Scenes involving citizens should reflect that heroes are public institutions; praise, fear, celebrity, and distrust all matter.
constraints: Keep the setting's social systems visible when Quirks, heroes, students, or civilians matter.
antiLore: Do not flatten MHA into generic superhero fantasy with no licensing, media, or social consequences.

entries.title: Rescue Is As Important As Combat

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Hero training and hero work value rescue, evacuation, judgment, and damage control alongside combat strength.
content.injection: Do not frame hero success only as defeating villains; rescue judgment and civilian protection are core hero measures.
constraints: Keep the setting's social systems visible when Quirks, heroes, students, or civilians matter.
antiLore: Do not flatten MHA into generic superhero fantasy with no licensing, media, or social consequences.

content.fact: Unlicensed public Quirk use can create legal trouble even when the user has good intentions.
content.injection: If a student or civilian uses a Quirk in public, account for licensing, self-defense, emergency context, and possible disciplinary consequences.
constraints: Keep the setting's social systems visible when Quirks, heroes, students, or civilians matter.
antiLore: Do not flatten MHA into generic superhero fantasy with no licensing, media, or social consequences.

content.fact: Hero names function as public brands, tactical identifiers, personal ideals, and professional labels.
content.injection: Use hero names as meaningful public identities, not just nicknames; students choosing names should reveal self-image and future branding.
constraints: Keep the setting's social systems visible when Quirks, heroes, students, or civilians matter.
antiLore: Do not flatten MHA into generic superhero fantasy with no licensing, media, or social consequences.

content.fact: Hero costumes combine combat function, Quirk support, safety design, recognizability, and public image.
content.injection: Treat costumes as designed tools that express identity and support Quirk use rather than simple fashion.
constraints: Keep the setting's social systems visible when Quirks, heroes, students, or civilians matter.

content.fact: Support gear can enhance or stabilize a hero's work, but it does not erase training limits, legal oversight, or Quirk drawbacks.
content.injection: Use support gear to solve specific tactical problems without turning students into fully optimized superheroes overnight.
constraints: Keep the setting's social systems visible when Quirks, heroes, students, or civilians matter.
antiLore: Do not flatten MHA into generic superhero fantasy with no licensing, media, or social consequences.

content.fact: Many Quirks impose practical drawbacks, bodily limits, environmental dependencies, social issues, or training needs.
content.injection: A character's Quirk should influence habits, risk, recovery, and social treatment even outside combat.
constraints: Keep the setting's social systems visible when Quirks, heroes, students, or civilians matter.

entries.title: Villain Labels Are Social And Legal

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: The label 'villain' carries legal danger, social stigma, and media framing; it is not merely a personal aesthetic.
content.injection: Use villain labels as public and legal categories that change how police, heroes, schools, and civilians react.
constraints: Keep the setting's social systems visible when Quirks, heroes, students, or civilians matter.
antiLore: Do not flatten MHA into generic superhero fantasy with no licensing, media, or social consequences.

content.fact: Hero society can fail people who are Quirkless, stigmatized, exploited, impoverished, abused, or unable to fit heroic norms.
content.injection: Do not portray the setting as uniformly fair; systemic pressure and neglected outsiders are recurring story forces.
constraints: Keep the setting's social systems visible when Quirks, heroes, students, or civilians matter.
antiLore: Do not flatten MHA into generic superhero fantasy with no licensing, media, or social consequences.

content.fact: Hero reputations are shaped by results, appearance, interviews, agency branding, scandals, and public narratives.
content.injection: Public scenes should account for media pressure and reputation management when heroes succeed, fail, or expose weakness.
constraints: Keep the setting's social systems visible when Quirks, heroes, students, or civilians matter.

content.fact: U.A. continues operating after crises, but villain attacks undermine the sense that school training is safely separated from real danger.
content.injection: After villain contact, keep student life functional but pressure-filled; normal classes must not erase trauma or security concern.
constraints: Keep the setting's social systems visible when Quirks, heroes, students, or civilians matter.
antiLore: Do not flatten MHA into generic superhero fantasy with no licensing, media, or social consequences.

content.fact: Hero rankings reward visibility, public approval, case resolution, and perceived strength, creating status pressure among pros.
content.injection: When pro heroes discuss status, treat rankings as a social and professional force, not a pure measure of virtue.
constraints: Keep the setting's social systems visible when Quirks, heroes, students, or civilians matter.

content.fact: Civilians often rely on heroes during disasters, but their trust can change when heroes seem absent, reckless, weak, or dishonest.
content.injection: In crisis scenes, civilian reactions should depend on recent events and visible hero reliability.
constraints: Keep the setting's social systems visible when Quirks, heroes, students, or civilians matter.

entries.title: Heroism Is A Moral Ideal And A Job

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: The story treats heroism as both a licensed career and an ideal of saving others beyond self-interest.
content.injection: Keep tension between professional hero work and genuine saving instinct active in character choices.
constraints: Keep the setting's social systems visible when Quirks, heroes, students, or civilians matter.
antiLore: Do not flatten MHA into generic superhero fantasy with no licensing, media, or social consequences.

content.fact: Students grow up seeing Quirks, hero news, villain incidents, and rescue imagery as part of ordinary life.
content.injection: Young characters can idolize danger while still underestimating real villain violence until they experience it.
constraints: Keep the setting's social systems visible when Quirks, heroes, students, or civilians matter.
antiLore: Do not flatten MHA into generic superhero fantasy with no licensing, media, or social consequences.
